news.headline: Tech Stocks Power S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Records
news.summary: Surging tech stocks carried the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite to new records on Wednesday, a milestone in major indexes’ rebound from war-fueled losses.  Buoyed by big gains from the likes of  Microsoft  and  Tesla  the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7022.95—rallying into the close to finish comfortably above its previous all-time high set in January.  More recently, though, investors have grown increasingly confident that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a peace deal.

news.headline: Marvell Eeks Out Another Record Close. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
news.summary: Shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell closed higher Wednesday after dipping more than 1% earlier in the day to notch their sixth consecutive day of gains and a new record close.  Booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains as well, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Schafer said Marvell is well-positioned because of its “full suite” of copper and optical products.

news.headline: Lilly Stock Dips on FDA Request for More Foundayo Safety Data. Is the Market Reaction Overblown?
news.summary: Eli Lilly  stock took a hit Wednesday after the Food and Drug Administration requested more safety data on the pharmaceutical giant’s weight-loss pill.  Lilly’s once-daily pill, sold under the brand name Foundayo, secured FDA approval earlier this month.  The drugmaker has positioned it as a contender to  Novo Nordisk’s  oral Wegovy, which became available in January, in a bid to deepen its lead in the market for GLP-1 drugs.
